Class InstantStopwatch

java.lang.Object
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.util.stopwatch.AbstractStopwatch
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.util.stopwatch.InstantStopwatch
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable, Stopwatch
public class InstantStopwatch extends AbstractStopwatch implements Stopwatch
A Stopwatch that uses Instant timestamp calculations

    Method
    Description
    void
    pause()
    Pauses the stopwatch.

    Starts or resumes the stopwatch.
    Instant
    startTime()
    Returns a timestamp representing the time this stopwatch was started, never null.
    void
    stop()
    Stops the stopwatch and clear its stats.
    long
    timeElapsed()
    Returns how much time elapsed since the stopwatch started (in milliseconds).

  • Constructor Details

    • InstantStopwatch

      public InstantStopwatch()
      Constructs a new InstantStopwatch.

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start()
      Starts or resumes the stopwatch.
      Specified by:
      start in interface Stopwatch
      Overrides:
      start in class AbstractStopwatch
      Throws:
      IllegalStopwatchStateException - if the stopwatch is already running.

    • pause

      public void pause()
      Pauses the stopwatch. This method doesn't clear the stopwatch stats, unlike the Stopwatch.stop() method.
      Specified by:
      pause in interface Stopwatch
      Overrides:
      pause in class AbstractStopwatch
      Throws:
      IllegalStopwatchStateException - if the stopwatch is not running.
      See Also:
      • Stopwatch.State.RUNNING

    • stop

      public void stop()
      Stops the stopwatch and clear its stats.
      Specified by:
      stop in interface Stopwatch
      Overrides:
      stop in class AbstractStopwatch
      Throws:
      IllegalStopwatchStateException - if the stopwatch is already stopped.

    • startTime

      public Instant startTime()
      Returns a timestamp representing the time this stopwatch was started, never null.
      Specified by:
      startTime in interface Stopwatch
      Returns:
      when this stopwatch was started
      Throws:
      IllegalStopwatchStateException - if Stopwatch.wasStarted() returns false.

    • timeElapsed

      public long timeElapsed()
      Returns how much time elapsed since the stopwatch started (in milliseconds). If Stopwatch.wasStarted() returns false, returns 0L.
      Specified by:
      timeElapsed in interface Stopwatch
      Returns:
      how much time elapsed since the stopwatch started
